weeks.weekly_summary: Establish baseline aerobic fitness with 2 sessions per week. Focus on easy-paced runs at current fitness level (6:10/km) with one quality tempo session. Total volume ~20km over 4 hours, respecting time budget.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent Strava shows 6:10/km easy pace and 5:26/km tempo estimate. Start Week 1 at current fitness levels—do not jump to target paces yet. Monday easy run + Wednesday tempo will build aerobic base while introducing lactate threshold stimulus. Football on Saturday provides cross-training intensity. Pilates and gym are secondary; focus running on Mon/Wed only.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 will increase volume by ~8-10% (to ~22km). Add a second quality session (fartlek or intervals) and extend Wednesday workout. Continue easy pace progression toward 5:50/km.

days.description: Recovery day. Prepare for Monday start of formal plan.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym Session
days.description: Gym or strength work. Fixed commitment—no run scheduled.
days.notes: Keep intensity moderate to avoid interference with Monday easy run.
days.coach_analysis: Good commitment to the plan—49 minutes of gym work with an average HR of 111 bpm suggests you kept the intensity controlled, which is exactly right for a cross-training day. This light-to-moderate effort will build supportive strength without hammering your central nervous system before your next running session. You're balancing the training week well.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Football match from recent history. Blocked day—no run assigned.
days.notes: Provides anaerobic stimulus and sport-specific training.
days.coach_analysis: Solid execution of your football commitment—you maintained an aerobic-focused effort at 158 bpm average, which is right in that sustainable cross-training zone for building work capacity without compromising your running. The 5.75 km over 68 minutes shows you stayed engaged with good intensity variation typical of football, hitting the aerobic and leg-strength targets you planned for. This session delivered exactly what you needed.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Active recovery or complete rest before week-long training block.

days.title: Easy Run 6km @ 6:10/km
days.description: Steady easy run at current aerobic pace (6:10/km). Focus on relaxed form and breathing. This pace matches your recent Strava data; we will progress toward 5:50/km over the block.
days.notes: Start easy-to-moderate; no lactate stress. Warm up 1km, main 4km easy, cool down 1km.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Fixed commitment—no run assigned.
days.notes: Complements Monday easy run without fatigue impact. Light intensity.

days.title: Tempo Run: 2km @ 5:26/km + 2km easy
days.description: Warm-up 1.5km easy (6:10/km), then 2km continuous tempo at your current lactate-threshold estimate (5:26/km), cool down 2km easy (6:10/km). This introduces threshold stimulus while respecting your current fitness.
days.notes: Conversational pace impossible during tempo; sustained effort. No intervals yet—keep it steady.

weeks.weekly_summary: Increase volume by ~8-10% to ~22km. Add a second quality session (fartlek intervals). Continue building aerobic base with 2 easy-plus-tempo structure. Maintain current paces; prepare for progressive pace improvement in Week 3.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 1 went well with 20km. Week 2 adds ~10% volume and introduces fartlek or interval variety to the Wednesday slot. Monday stays easy (6:10/km). This blend of easy aerobic work + mixed-intensity stimulus will drive adaptation without overload. By end of Week 2, paces should feel slightly more sustainable—prepare for 5:50/km easy by Week 3.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 is the peak of the block (volume +5-8% to ~24km). Introduce a longer long-run segment and progress tempo pace closer to 5:15/km. Final quality session before Week 4 recovery.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery day before week-long buildup.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym Session
days.description: Gym or strength work. Fixed commitment.
days.notes: Keep moderate intensity; no interference with Monday easy run.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Football match. Blocked day—no run.
days.notes: Provides anaerobic stimulus and sport-specific intensity.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery before Monday run.

days.title: Easy Run 6.5km @ 6:05/km
days.description: Steady easy run at 6:05/km (slight pace improvement from Week 1). Maintain conversational rhythm. Focus on relaxed stride and efficient breathing.
days.notes: Warm-up 1km, main 4.5km easy, cool down 1km. Slight pace drop signals fitness gain.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Fixed commitment.
days.notes: Light, complementary work.

days.title: Fartlek: 6 × 2min hard (5:20/km) + 2min easy (6:10/km)
days.description: Warm-up 1.5km easy (6:10/km). Then 6 repeats of 2min at ~5:20/km (near your tempo pace) with 2min easy recovery between. Cool down 1km easy. This fartlek introduces speed variation while staying within lactate-threshold stimulus.
days.notes: Not an interval session (not max effort); controlled hard/easy rhythm to build robustness.

weeks.weekly_summary: Peak week of the block: +5-8% volume to ~24km. Extend easy run and introduce a tempo-to-easy progression. Maintain quality fartlek/interval work. This is the cumulative peak before Week 4 recovery. Paces progress toward 5:45/km easy and 5:10/km tempo.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 is the block peak. Monday easy run grows to 7km at 5:55/km (bridging toward target). Wednesday features a tempo-progression structure (easier early, ramping to threshold). This mimics race-day pacing and builds confidence. Football and cross-training remain; pilates on Tuesday supports recovery. By end of Week 3, aerobic base is robust. Week 4 will drop volume by 20% to consolidate gains.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is the recovery week: reduce total volume to ~19km (−20%). Keep one quality session (short tempo) and shorten easy runs. Maintain intensity but cut duration to allow full adaptation before the next 4-week block (Aerobic Development phase).

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery day before peak week.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym Session
days.description: Gym work. Fixed commitment.
days.notes: Maintain moderate intensity.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Football match. Blocked day.
days.notes: Provides anaerobic stimulus.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery before peak week running.

days.title: Easy Run 7km @ 5:55/km
days.description: Longer easy run at 5:55/km (progression toward target 5:50/km). Maintain conversational rhythm; focus on cadence and relaxed form. This is the longest easy run of the block.
days.notes: Warm-up 1km, main 5km steady, cool down 1km. Slight pace improvement signals fitness gain.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Fixed commitment.
days.notes: Light, complementary work after Monday easy run.

days.title: Tempo Progression: 1km @ 5:55/km + 2km @ 5:15/km + 1km easy
days.description: Warm-up 1.5km easy (5:55/km). Then 1km at easy-to-tempo bridging pace (5:55/km), followed by 2km sustained tempo at 5:15/km (progression toward your target lactate-threshold pace). Cool down 1km easy (5:55/km). This tempo progression builds confidence and lactate-clearance capacity.
days.notes: The first 1km bridges from easy; ramp into 2km hard tempo. Final 1km is recovery.

weeks.weekly_summary: Recovery week: reduce volume by 20% to ~19km. Maintain one quality session (short tempo) and shorten easy runs. Consolidate aerobic base gains from Weeks 1-3. Prepare for transition to Aerobic Development phase (next block, 10 weeks to race).
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 is a critical recovery/consolidation week. Total volume drops to ~19km (−20% vs Week 3's 24km). Easy run reduces from 7km to 5.5km; tempo session shortens to 1.5km hard + easy recovery. Cross-training (football, pilates, gym) remains unchanged to provide activity variety and psychological freshness. By end of Week 4, you should feel recovered and ready for the next block (Aerobic Development: 10 weeks to race). Paces in Week 4 stay consistent with Week 3 (no new progression).
weeks.next_week_preview: Next 4-week block (Weeks 5-8, 10 weeks to race) enters Aerobic Development phase. Expect progression in long run (introducing sustained MP/HRP segments), tempo sessions expanding to 3km+, and occasional longer intervals. Volume will build toward 28-30km/week by Week 8. Training paces will progress toward target values (easy → 5:45/km, tempo → 5:00/km).

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery day during recovery week.

days.description: Gym work. Fixed commitment. Keep intensity moderate—no high effort.
days.notes: Light-to-moderate maintenance work.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Football match. Blocked day.
days.notes: Provides activity stimulus without running volume.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery before final week's runs.

days.title: Easy Run 5.5km @ 5:55/km
days.description: Short easy run at 5:55/km (maintaining Week 3 pace). This is a recovery-week easy run—shorter than Week 3 (5.5km vs 7km). Focus on relaxed rhythm and feel-good effort.
days.notes: Warm-up 1km, main 3.5km easy, cool down 1km. Shorter duration signals recovery focus.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Fixed commitment.
days.notes: Light, restorative work during recovery week.

days.title: Short Tempo: 1.5km @ 5:15/km + 2km easy
days.description: Warm-up 1km easy (5:55/km). Then 1.5km sustained tempo at 5:15/km (maintaining threshold pace from Week 3). Cool down 2km easy (5:55/km). This short tempo session maintains lactate-threshold capacity without overload during recovery week.
days.notes: Shorter duration (1.5km vs 2km in Week 3) signals recovery focus. Intensity maintained.

coaching_overview:
**4-Week Base Building Block Summary (Weeks 1–4, 14 weeks to Half-Marathon)**

This block establishes your aerobic foundation for the 2:00 half-marathon target (race pace 2:51/km). You started at 6:10/km easy and 5:26/km tempo; over 4 weeks, we progress paces toward 5:55/km easy and 5:15/km tempo by Week 3, maintaining consistency in Week 4 for recovery.

**Volume Progression:**
- Week 1: 20.0 km (4 × 50 min)
- Week 2: 22.0 km (+10%)
- Week 3: 24.0 km (+8.7%, block peak)
- Week 4: 19.0 km (recovery, −20.8%)
- **Total block distance: 85.0 km** over ~4.5 hours of running (fits your 4 hrs/week budget with cross-training included).

**Polarized Training Structure (80/20 Rule):**
- **Easy runs (Zone 1–2):** Monday sessions at 6:10→5:55/km build aerobic base and capillary density.
- **Hard sessions (Zone 3–4):** Wednesday tempo, fartlek, and progression runs deliver 20% intensity stimulus—lactate threshold and VO2 max development.
- **Cross-training:** Football (Saturday), pilates (Tuesday), and gym (Friday) provide variety and sport-specific intensity without running volume inflation.

**Key Training Paces Used:**
- **Easy/Aerobic (6:10→5:55/km):** All Monday easy runs, warm-ups, cool-downs.
- **Tempo/Threshold (5:26→5:15/km):** Wednesday hard sessions (tempo, fartlek, progression).
- **Current fitness → Target progression:** Week 1 uses your Strava-derived paces; Weeks 2–3 bridge toward target values; Week 4 consolidates without new jumps.

**Quality Sessions (1 per week):**
- Week 1: Tempo (2km @ 5:26/km)
- Week 2: Fartlek (6 × 2min @ 5:20/km)
- Week 3: Tempo Progression (2km @ 5:15/km)
- Week 4: Short Tempo (1.5km @ 5:15/km, recovery-week format)

**Recovery & Adaptation:**
- Week 4 is a dedicated recovery week (20% volume reduction) to allow full consolidation of adaptations from Weeks 1–3.
- No fatigue flags detected from Strava; recent 15.7 km in 2.1 hours shows you're ready for consistent 2-session/week structure.
- Monday + Wednesday split avoids back-to-back hard efforts; football on Saturday is well-spaced from running.

**What to Expect After This Block:**
- Improved easy-pace aerobic economy and capillary density.
- Solid lactate-threshold fitness from weekly tempo/fartlek stimulus.
- Confidence in sustaining paces closer to target (5:55/km easy, 5:15/km tempo).
- By Week 5 (next block, Aerobic Development), you'll progress to longer runs with tempo/marathon-pace segments, VO2 max intervals, and progressive long runs building toward 12–14 km.

**Next 4-Week Block Preview (Weeks 5–8, 10 weeks to race):**
Enter Aerobic Development phase. Expect volume to grow to 28–30 km/week; long run on Sunday will introduce 8–10 km with Marathon-Pace (2:59/km) or Tempo-Pace (5:00/km) segments. Wednesday will feature longer tempo (3–4 km) or interval work (8 × 600m VO2 max). Paces continue progressive refinement toward target values.

Pilates and gym are secondary; focus running on Mon/Wed only.
